original text:
Soon after started internship Caster, realized that all the communications and public relations classes I'd taken didn't teach everything that would come across real-world environment. Here was introduced new types projects and industry resources, and one thing I've done lot create media lists. didn't know anything about media lists before being intern Caster, and had never learned how put one together. first instinct about media lists was that they were Word documents you created with media sources that pertained the topic hand. had idea how detailed they needed and how much useful information you could actually find. Luckily for have great team that eased into this process and taught how correctly put one together.
Media lists are compilation names, information and sources that are applicable campaigns and outreach for media pitching. These names include specific journalists and outlets who have written currently write about the topic you want pitch. For example, you're looking for coverage about new tech gadget your client has released, you may want pitch those who write for TechCrunch Engadget, who covered similar product the past. The media list will usually consist the name journalist, outlet they write for, email contact information, Twitter handle and link something relevant they've written. has taken little while figure out which targets are good for certain pitch and should added list. I've learned that you want your target post regularly about the topic you are pitching. they have only posted about certain topic once, you're better off finding something else. your target has large following and respected outlet the industry you are working in, that also good sign.
important key compiling good media list making sure that all the information you use up-to-date. sure that the journalist you include still working for the outlet and writing about topics that are your area interest. Another important detail watch out for the journalists' email address, since addresses that are news@site.com and are just for general use and are ineffective for pitching. resource that use find this information Meltwater, which has search function where you can look for journalists. they are the system, Meltwater provides you with updated information for that journalist. someone you are looking for not Meltwater you must search through the web. suggest Googling their name and checking Twitter, LinkedIn and their personal sites for contact information.
Media lists are crucial part pitching help your clients get coverage. keep working media lists, become better and better. has become lot easier for spot good targets and have even begun recognize big names the tech-writing business. It's great feeling knowing that your research being used effective way help get client coverage. Keep mind the tips and tricks shared above from personal experience and let know what you think connecting with Twitter @AliCerasuolo!

original text:
The Centre for the Development Christian Ministry Trinity Theological College (TTC) which organises courses equip Christians for lifelong learning, personal growth and ministry will offering two evening courses from September November 2016.
This historical survey the Reformation. The course traces the circumstances that gave rise the Reformation and the different religious, political and cultural forces that coalesced birthing the Protestant church. will examine the lives and legacies some the prominent reformers and the implications Karl Barth's comment that "the church always reformed" (Ecclesia semper reformanda est) for our present times.
Instructor: Rev. Dr. Andrew Peh faculty Trinity Theological College and lectures the area mission and mission history. alumni the college (TTC) and Asbury Theological Seminary and his interests are the history Christianity Asia and mission trends the 21st century. also serves the Chinese Annual Conference the Methodist Church Singapore diaconal minister appointed Charis Methodist Church.
Various ethical issues challenge Christians today: some result the increased sophistication and pervasiveness technology, others are dilemmas which arise because Christian values are variance with the world's. Since these issues are often contentious and divisive, Christians need develop theological framework able salt and light society, with posture humility and grace articulating the Christian view. This course will give the opportunity and grow this knowledge and develop these skills.
Instructor: Dr. Kwa Kiem Kiok teaches East Asia School Theology the areas Intercultural Studies well ethics. She has worked both para-church organisations well her home church, Trinity Methodist Church. When she's not working, she enjoys listening classical music and reading detective novels.
For more information, please visit www.ttc.edu.sg contact Esther Lee telephone +65 67613624 Email cdcm@ttc.edu.sg. register, click here.

original text:
Today would like talk about yet another unique embroidery tradition the Dnister corridor, This river separates the historic districts Pokuttia and Podillia. This region incredibly rich embroidery techniques. This technique mostly known for being used the ends the ancient woman's headpiece, the peremitka, which long narrow piece linen which wound around the head and neck married women. The ends this garment were always decorated either with woven design with embroidery. These ornemented ends were called 'zabir'. The edges were sometimes also ornamented, usually with simple woven designs. Here are some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky.
This type embroidery was used women's chemises well, but lasted longest the zabory because could made look almost the same both sides.
The image the top the article good example this technique, even though the photo somewhat washed out. will using several images which are rather poor quality, and apologize that was not able find better.
The framework for these designs was laid out black cross stich, sometimes stem stich. For the zabir, double sided cross stitch was used. Then colors were filled with herringbone pine type stitch, which started from the center line the outlined area and extended diagonally the outer edge. This was often executed silk thread, and sometimes metallic thread accent..Some the outlines, and often decorative elements the edge the design were done with Holbein stitch.Other stitches were used for the edging, including buttonhole and chain stitch.
Elements which are equilateral end looking almost like eyelets, but they are not pulled open. The primary colors used are dark reds, edging into maroon and purple with other colors used accents.
Some designs use stem stitch instead cross stitch, and are curvilinear. The filling done the same way however.
Many designs are symmetrical about the diagonal axis, which similar embroidery designs the Balkans.
And course, wherever creative embroiderers existed, variations the technique also existed.
More recently, this technique, with many others, was imitated with cross-stitch. Here are two shoulder insets from women's chemises with the same design, one done rozchokhy, and the other cross stitch.
And another pair, from zabory.
fact, belief that many designs this area with strong black outlines individual motifs which are today only found cross stitch were originally executed rozchokha. would interesting reverse engineer these designs.
suppose should mention that some these designs were deliberately left unfinished more clearly show the structure.
Occasionally you will see remnant rozchokha cross stitch design.
This technique has today been revived, and being taught places like the Uchylyshche L'viw. New designs using the technique are also being invented. had such piece personal collection, but was borrowed and never returned.
This beautiful part the creative heritage Ukraine and the world, and would encourage you try it. This technique has great deal potential.
Thank you for reading. will close with some more images pieces done this technique.
Hello, Roman. Thanks for this post! thought, that Pokuttya peremitkas was mainly woven (like this from Jaseniv-Pilny but your post opened eyes the wonderful local version embroidered peremitkas. don't have the 3rd Iryna Svyontek's book, ask you, there the book how this embroidery?
the way, "some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky" are the folk band "Peremitka" from village Luka, Horodenka raion and you can watch their performance youtube
Thank you, Nawko, would like know where could find her other two books. have not found them available here.
She does not show how this embroidery, but you can save and enlarge the black and white images and able see how done.It pretty cross stitch and outline stitch, filling with herringbone sosnytsia. Most books Ukrainian embroidery cover this, for example the one Diakiw neil. called plait stitch, peremitkovy show Horodenkovy stib, somesuch. There description, diagrams 'Podil'ska Narodna Vyshywka Liudmyla
Thank you for your information. and please let know how could buy her other two books.
Перші присвячені Гуцульщині, одну них маю книжкою (2-гу)(поталанило, привезли іншу найстаршу скановану копію форматі djvu. Києві їх видно продажу, видно
Ті, бачила, зібрання чорно-білих схем низинкових розводів (переважно). Нагадують книжку Сороханюк, але жаль, гіршої якості. маєте знати, поліграфічна якість книжок пані Свйонтек покращувалась кожним виданням та, маєте найкраща, давніші трохи поступаються.
знайшла видавництво, видало дві трьох книжок, "Нова Зоря", ними можна сконтактувати електронною поштою nz@com.if.ua.
Також, ймовірно, усі книжки авторки, але її контактів нажаль маю.
один коментар українською, усе, хочу, моя погана англійська дозволить написати (сподіваюсь, мене зрозумієте). Можливо, коли матимете час, Вас зацікавить тема, над якою зараз думаю білі вишивки Житомирщини. (Баранівський район, Суємці навколишні) наполегливо шукаю книги статті, виділяли вишивку, але поки марно. Єдине джерело, містить замальовки "Український народний орнамент" Олени Пчілки, 1876 (починаючи аркуша). Завдяки вишивальниці Тетяні можемо переглядати видання онлайн тут Цей альбом містить зразки орнаментів повіту, якого Баранівський район належав, але містить конкретики щодо місць побутування кожного окремого узору також мість дуже загальний опис вишивки вирізуванням настилуванням без виділення якихось локальних рис.
Усе інше, вдалось знайти фото вишивок Мережі. Велику підбірку фондах музею Гончара пані Олена Уткіна. Її фото можна знайти альбомі іншої вишивальниці, пані Лідії Бебешко, яка перетворює їх PM-схеми:
Усі інші зразки, мені вдалось знайти, зібрала дошку Pinterest: (звідти можна потрапити першоджерела, якщо були соціальні медіа). Ось все.
Pereproshuju shcho vam vidpyshu latinikamy. chudovi znymky knyshchi 'Vyshywka Skhidnoho Podillia', pysaly Jevhen Tetiana Prychepi, vydawnytsa 'Rodovid'.
Так, звичайно, читала Ваші статті. Але тут мене зацікавило Житомирське біле шитво, оремий різновид українських вишивок білим білому. скільки бачу воно якраз мало схоже вінницьке снятинське, більше схоже полтавське, хоча має ряд особливостей, які якраз перераховувала пунктиками :), хоча мало бути більше схоже рівненське (сарненський район), туди ближче. Перепрошую турботу.
majete uvazi Podil'ske vyrizuvannia. Tak, maju kil'ky fotohrafii tseho takozh. Mih vam posylaty jakscho meni daiete adress email.
Interesting and exhaustive articles here,thank you!
very modern view things you have. Embroidery Ukraine many other places was considered essential. was, and some extent still is, visible mark your good taste, how marriageable you are, and evidence proper householding skills. Untill about the 1930's, all the cloth and thread for embroidered items were made home, hand. Today pay exorbitant prices for good linen, but those days, was much more available. Even when commercial thread began used, was never that expensive. Secondly, these clothes were not worn for season and then discarded, unfortunately today. Linen extremely durable, and embroidered garment could worn for decades. This especially true for the most extensively embroidered garments which were only worn for important occasions, perhaps for church Sunday, most. Everyday clothes would eventually wear out, and had less embroidery, but even then would recycled footcloths, baby swaddling and the like. This was not much done city women untill the 1930's when became fashionable have ethnic identity. The woman the villages worked the family farm, and for much the year agricultural work does leave one with great deal free time, excepting only planting time and harvest. Also, one the most important things that young girls were expected was prepare their trousseau; girl was not considered ready for marriage untill she had proper collection embroidered clothes and household linens prepared, often including shirt for her future spouse. Women would also embroider shirts for their husbands and sons. was considered extremely strong protective talisman for young man out into the world with shirt embroidered his mother. Embroidery was never considered just hobby', formed vital part life. This has not completely been lost Ukrainian culture even today. And course, all this applies many other peoples well.
Ohh,thank you, Roman; very informative.
Hi.. Great post. Well you have discussed here few things about Costume Embroidery and your images also looks amazing. Keep this type posting.

Today would like talk about yet another unique embroidery tradition the Dnister corridor, This river separates the historic districts Pokuttia and Podillia. This region incredibly rich embroidery techniques. This technique mostly known for being used the ends the ancient woman's headpiece, the peremitka, which long narrow piece linen which wound around the head and neck married women. The ends this garment were always decorated either with woven design with embroidery. These ornemented ends were called 'zabir'. The edges were sometimes also ornamented, usually with simple woven designs. Here are some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky.
This type embroidery was used women's chemises well, but lasted longest the zabory because could made look almost the same both sides.
The image the top the article good example this technique, even though the photo somewhat washed out. will using several images which are rather poor quality, and apologize that was not able find better.
The framework for these designs was laid out black cross stich, sometimes stem stich. For the zabir, double sided cross stitch was used. Then colors were filled with herringbone pine type stitch, which started from the center line the outlined area and extended diagonally the outer edge. This was often executed silk thread, and sometimes metallic thread accent..Some the outlines, and often decorative elements the edge the design were done with Holbein stitch.Other stitches were used for the edging, including buttonhole and chain stitch.
Elements which are equilateral end looking almost like eyelets, but they are not pulled open. The primary colors used are dark reds, edging into maroon and purple with other colors used accents.
Some designs use stem stitch instead cross stitch, and are curvilinear. The filling done the same way however.
Many designs are symmetrical about the diagonal axis, which similar embroidery designs the Balkans.
And course, wherever creative embroiderers existed, variations the technique also existed.
More recently, this technique, with many others, was imitated with cross-stitch. Here are two shoulder insets from women's chemises with the same design, one done rozchokhy, and the other cross stitch.
And another pair, from zabory.
fact, belief that many designs this area with strong black outlines individual motifs which are today only found cross stitch were originally executed rozchokha. would interesting reverse engineer these designs.
suppose should mention that some these designs were deliberately left unfinished more clearly show the structure.
Occasionally you will see remnant rozchokha cross stitch design.
This technique has today been revived, and being taught places like the Uchylyshche L'viw. New designs using the technique are also being invented. had such piece personal collection, but was borrowed and never returned.
This beautiful part the creative heritage Ukraine and the world, and would encourage you try it. This technique has great deal potential.
Thank you for reading. will close with some more images pieces done this technique.
Hello, Roman. Thanks for this post! thought, that Pokuttya peremitkas was mainly woven (like this from Jaseniv-Pilny but your post opened eyes the wonderful local version embroidered peremitkas. don't have the 3rd Iryna Svyontek's book, ask you, there the book how this embroidery?
the way, "some women Horoden'ka district costume wearing peremitky" are the folk band "Peremitka" from village Luka, Horodenka raion and you can watch their performance youtube
Thank you, Nawko, would like know where could find her other two books. have not found them available here.
She does not show how this embroidery, but you can save and enlarge the black and white images and able see how done.It pretty cross stitch and outline stitch, filling with herringbone sosnytsia. Most books Ukrainian embroidery cover this, for example the one Diakiw neil. called plait stitch, peremitkovy show Horodenkovy stib, somesuch. There description, diagrams 'Podil'ska Narodna Vyshywka Liudmyla
Thank you for your information. and please let know how could buy her other two books.
Перші присвячені Гуцульщині, одну них маю книжкою (2-гу)(поталанило, привезли іншу найстаршу скановану копію форматі djvu. Києві їх видно продажу, видно
Ті, бачила, зібрання чорно-білих схем низинкових розводів (переважно). Нагадують книжку Сороханюк, але жаль, гіршої якості. маєте знати, поліграфічна якість книжок пані Свйонтек покращувалась кожним виданням та, маєте найкраща, давніші трохи поступаються.
знайшла видавництво, видало дві трьох книжок, "Нова Зоря", ними можна сконтактувати електронною поштою .
Також, ймовірно, усі книжки авторки, але її контактів нажаль маю.
один коментар українською, усе, хочу, моя погана англійська дозволить написати (сподіваюсь, мене зрозумієте). Можливо, коли матимете час, Вас зацікавить тема, над якою зараз думаю білі вишивки Житомирщини. (Баранівський район, Суємці навколишні) наполегливо шукаю книги статті, виділяли вишивку, але поки марно. Єдине джерело, містить замальовки "Український народний орнамент" Олени Пчілки, 1876 (починаючи аркуша). Завдяки вишивальниці Тетяні можемо переглядати видання онлайн тут Цей альбом містить зразки орнаментів повіту, якого Баранівський район належав, але містить конкретики щодо місць побутування кожного окремого узору також мість дуже загальний опис вишивки вирізуванням настилуванням без виділення якихось локальних рис.
Усе інше, вдалось знайти фото вишивок Мережі. Велику підбірку фондах музею Гончара пані Олена Уткіна. Її фото можна знайти альбомі іншої вишивальниці, пані Лідії Бебешко, яка перетворює їх PM-схеми:
Усі інші зразки, мені вдалось знайти, зібрала дошку Pinterest: (звідти можна потрапити першоджерела, якщо були соціальні медіа). Ось все.
Pereproshuju shcho vam vidpyshu latinikamy. chudovi znymky knyshchi 'Vyshywka Skhidnoho Podillia', pysaly Jevhen Tetiana Prychepi, vydawnytsa 'Rodovid'.
Так, звичайно, читала Ваші статті. Але тут мене зацікавило Житомирське біле шитво, оремий різновид українських вишивок білим білому. скільки бачу воно якраз мало схоже вінницьке снятинське, більше схоже полтавське, хоча має ряд особливостей, які якраз перераховувала пунктиками :), хоча мало бути більше схоже рівненське (сарненський район), туди ближче. Перепрошую турботу.
majete uvazi Podil'ske vyrizuvannia. Tak, maju kil'ky fotohrafii tseho takozh. Mih vam posylaty jakscho meni daiete adress email.
Interesting and exhaustive articles here,thank you!
very modern view things you have. Embroidery Ukraine many other places was considered essential. was, and some extent still is, visible mark your good taste, how marriageable you are, and evidence proper householding skills. Untill about the 1930's, all the cloth and thread for embroidered items were made home, hand. Today pay exorbitant prices for good linen, but those days, was much more available. Even when commercial thread began used, was never that expensive. Secondly, these clothes were not worn for season and then discarded, unfortunately today. Linen extremely durable, and embroidered garment could worn for decades. This especially true for the most extensively embroidered garments which were only worn for important occasions, perhaps for church Sunday, most. Everyday clothes would eventually wear out, and had less embroidery, but even then would recycled footcloths, baby swaddling and the like. This was not much done city women untill the 1930's when became fashionable have ethnic identity. The woman the villages worked the family farm, and for much the year agricultural work does leave one with great deal free time, excepting only planting time and harvest. Also, one the most important things that young girls were expected was prepare their trousseau; girl was not considered ready for marriage untill she had proper collection embroidered clothes and household linens prepared, often including shirt for her future spouse. Women would also embroider shirts for their husbands and sons. was considered extremely strong protective talisman for young man out into the world with shirt embroidered his mother. Embroidery was never considered just hobby', formed vital part life. This has not completely been lost Ukrainian culture even today. And course, all this applies many other peoples well.
Ohh,thank you, Roman; very informative.
Hi.. Great post. Well you have discussed here few things about Costume Embroidery and your images also looks amazing. Keep this type posting.

original text:
Mayor James will participate the Conference alongside leaders from other states.
FORWARD THIS EMAIL ANYONE INTERESTED THE LATEST WPB NEWS.
You're Invited the Curbside Chat!
Join Mayor Jeri Muoio and Strong Towns Founder Chuck Marohn for Curbside Chat! Marohn, whose organization teaches communities how more financially resilient through smart planning and infrastructure use, will lead public presentation about how cities and neighborhoods can grow stronger taking incremental, data-responsive, steps through the work strong citizens. Hear how can leverage our transportation and mobility plan strengthen our community! Q&A will follow.
About Mr. Marohn: Marohn, noted professional engineer and certified planner, the lead author "Thoughts Building Strong Towns" well the author World Class Transportation System". hosts the Strong Towns Podcast and primary writer for Strong Towns' web content. has presented the Strong Towns concepts hundreds cities and towns across North America and 2017 was named one the Most Influential Urbanists all time Planetizen. learn more about Mr. Marohn and Strong Towns, visit:
The City West Palm Beach planning hit out the park honor the 2017 World Series Champion Houston Astros welcoming them back their Spring Training home with much fanfare and excitement. Thursday, February 22, during special edition the City's legacy event, Clematis Night, players and representatives from the Houston Astros will make special appearance. The event will held the Waterfront from p.m., and special experiences will include selfies with large-scale replica the World Series Trophy, opportunity see the actual World Series Trophy, appearances Astros mascot Orbit and the Astros Shooting Stars promotional team, and more. Remarks Mayor Jeri Muoio and members the World Champion club begin p.m. and the first 500 attendees won't need buy their Cracker Jacks hotdogs they will provided the City part the festive celebration. The special welcome for the first 500 attendees will also include promotional item from the Astros.
"We are thrilled welcome the Houston Astros back their Spring Training home and especially after such exciting 2017 season," said Mayor Muoio. "We are delighted honor them Clematis Night and celebrate not only their World Series success but also the role that West Palm Beach played helping them start out their championship season the right foot."
Spring Training officially starts the following day, February 23, when the Houston Astros take the National League East Champion Washington Nationals, who also call the Palm Beaches their Spring Training home, The Ballpark the Palm Beaches.
Clematis Night attendees are encouraged dress Astros jersey, the team's colors navy and orange. Guests will have opportunity win tickets Houston Astros Spring Training game The Ballpark the Palm Beaches. The special baseball-themed, weekly concert series will also include music New Horizon Band, who play R&B and top tunes.
Clematis Night include: The Palm Beach Post, 97.9 WRMF, 103.1 WIRK, SUNNY 107.9, 102.3, 850 AM, 640AM, Beatz, the West Palm Beach Community Redevelopment Agency, and the West Palm Beach Marriott.
Clematis Night held the West Palm Beach waterfront 101 Clematis Street. The weekly concert series produced The City West Palm Beach Department Parks and Recreation, Division Community Events.
For more information, including list bands and activities future events, please visit Wpb.org/events, call 561-822-1515, follow the City West Palm Beach Facebook @CityofWPB, Twitter @westpalmbch Instagram @westpalmbch.
During the month February, the City West Palm Beach will observe Black History Month, annual celebration that recognizes and honors the contributions, achievements and history the African American community. The public invited particpate any the FREE City events recognizing Black History Month. For complete list events, please visit:
The City West Palm Beach wants ensure that residents have the correct updates about the plans for Flagler Shore.
Recently, series private emails and texts containing inaccurate information about the City's Flagler Shore pilot project have created some new questions.
Flagler Shore was designed temporarily reduce Flagler Drive between Banyan Boulevard and Lakeview Avenue reclaim 63,000 square feet public space for the public benefit.
The Flagler Shore project will end, and all four lanes Flagler Drive will reopen March Please note that the City will need close the affected area Flagler Drive the final days the project order apply the MOT (Maintenance Traffic). Therefore, February 27, 28, there will full road closure Flagler Drive between Banyan and Lakeview. Please note that, past years, Flagler Drive may temporarily close for private events including the Boat Show and Sunfest.
Despite inaccurate reports the contrary, public dollars are being allocated continue fund the Flagler Shore project.
Flagler Shore has been successful starting the dialogue about how best utilize the City's public spaces and was part Mayor Muoio's initiative study how improve the overall quality life across the City.
While there are plans study the reports and review the results Flagler Shore, the project not listed one Mayor's top priorities.
This the plan and has always been the plan.
We've learned lot about how our residents value our public spaces. Project managers are compiling report, which will made public and shared with everyone once the project complete.
thank everyone who has participated the project and has taken the time provide their feedback and comments! For more information about Flagler Shore share with your feedback, please visit:
Coffee and bikes hand hand. Break out the Monday blues, perk and explore our downtown all before going work. Meet the pergola Flagler Shore 6:50 a.m., and begin easy bike ride along Flagler Shore ending the pergola a.m. Make your brew the spot B.Y.O.B. (bring your own brew). Morning coffee outside will conclude a.m.
lunch plans? problem! Bring your own lunch pre-order food from our downtown restaurants and have delivered the shore. There will also food trucks the south end 501 Flagler!
Start your Saturdays with healthy ride through Flagler Shore and enjoy the West Palm Beach GreenMarket. Ride the complimentary valet stationed across from the Green Lawn the waterfront the median. Need some air your tires, have flat tire, brakes gears don't work? problem! bicycle tech with supplies will happy fix your ride.
Please join our community walks engage our brothers and sisters!
Our next walk will take place February 14th 5:30pm!
For more information, contact Kevin Jones (561) 822-1413.
Photo Contest: Send Your Best Grassy Waters Shot!
Love nature and photography? Submit your amateur photos for chance win! The City West Palm Beach Grassy Waters Preserve Nature Center and the Grassy Waters Conservancy are hosting photography contest showcase the beauty the Grassy Waters Preserve West Palm Beach.
The goal the contest showcase the natural environment Grassy Waters Preserve. The Preserve, maintained the City's Public Utilities Department, 23-square-mile wetlands ecosystem that serves the freshwater supply for more than 100,000 area residents. All photos submitted for the contest must taken Grassy Waters Preserve. Images should reflect the diversity the Preserve including wildlife, natural habitats, animal behavior, plant life, water, weather, and seasons.
Come visit the Preserve and test your photography skills you explore the wetlands, tree islands and forested hammocks. You may able use some the commonly sighted species like alligators, Everglades snail kites, wading birds your photograph subjects! New for 2018 Smartphone category!
Visit the Grassy Waters Conservancy's website for more information, enter your photos and vote for your favorites.
The contest runs through February 28, 2018. For the complete 2018 rules, please visit:
Save the date! Join Saturday, April 14, 2018 for the Great American Cleanup Comcast Cares Day from Noon 4:00 P.M. Dreher Park South (off Summit Blvd. Meet the Main Pavilion #3)!
Let's create positive change and lasting impact Dreher Park! Join the nation's largest community improvement program, engaging more than million volunteers and participants every year.
Work completed: painting fencing and rental pavilions, and trimming weed vegetation and trees. Volunteer required. Individuals ages 8+, families and groups are encouraged participate. There will thank you gifts for all volunteers plus on-site refreshments!
For more information, contact the Division Volunteer Services (561) 804-4906 email rbarona@wpb.org.
The next meeting the City Commission will February 12, 2018 beginning 5pm.
The City West Palm Beach will hold its General Election Tuesday, March 13, 2018, for the purpose electing three City Commissioners: one residing District one residing District and one residing District
District will not appear the ballot, Commissioner Paula Ryan was unopposed and the for District
Residents ALL districts who are eligible vote can vote this election!
Thanksgiving Day, November 22, 2018; Residential garbage will resume your next scheduled service day beginning 11/26/18. Recycling will resume 11/29/18. Bulk/Yard and Commercial Divisions are one day behind regular schedule.
Christmas Day, December 25, 2018: Residential garbage will resume your next scheduled service day beginning 12/28/18. Recycling will resume 1/1/19. Bulk/Yard and Commercial Divisions are one day behind regular schedule.
New Year's Day, January 2019: Residential garbage will resume your next scheduled service day beginning 1/4/19. Recycling will operate normal schedule. Bulk/Yard and Commercial Divisions are one day behind regular schedule.
The sanitation schedule produced the City West Palm Beach Department Public Works. For more information, please call (561) 822-2075.
Please note that this schedule tentative and subject change. For more information, please call (561) 822-1412.
Public Works Request for Service:

original text:
The landscape development finance changing rapidly. Traditionally, international financial flows moved from developed countries developing countries. the last decade, however, major emerging economies such China and Brazil have fueled growing trend South-South development flows increasingly channeling their overseas investments other developing countries.
China and Brazil are surfacing major international investors through nationally owned financial institutions such the Export-Import Bank China, the China Development Bank and the Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES). These "emerging actors" are financing major initiatives acquire natural resources, open markets, and forge strategic political ties. They are increasingly financing large-scale, high impact projects beyond their borders such hydropower plants and gas pipelines which may pose new challenges for environmental and social sustainability.
This preliminary research focuses Chinese and Brazilian overseas investments and begins look the growth drivers and geographic trends those investments.
Expanding South-South trade and investment provides welcome and needed sources capital for countries Africa, Asia, and Latin America. the same time, these financial flows coupled with the emergence powerful financial actors from China, India, Brazil, and other economies may pose new challenges for environmental and social sustainability.
Trends indicate that developing economies will "account for 57% world GDP [Gross Domestic Product] 2030."3 Despite sharp differences among members, the G-20 supplanting the G-8 the primary vehicle for global economic policy coordination.4 Large emerging market economies are defining their own approaches development cooperation, governance issues, and environmental and social sustainability outside many existing normative frameworks.
2001, China's tenth Five-Year Plan (2001-2005) formalized the directive for Chinese companies "Go Global," strategy gain access needed resources, stimulate the export goods, and grow China's multinational businesses and brands. Beijing has provided diplomatic support, favorable tax exemptions, insurance, and, critically, access low-cost finance.
Chinese authorities have simplified regulations facilitate investment abroad. Three governmental bodies MOFCOM, SAFE, and the NDRC have primary but not sole oversight China's overseas investment (separate from foreign assistance) regime. MOFCOM responsible for developing regulations for outbound investment and for coordinating activities with commercial counselors posted Chinese embassies. SAFE issued new regulations 2009 that reduced qualification requirements for offshore foreign currency lending and expanded the sources funds for lending (including access government foreign exchange reserves).11 The NDRC reviews large outbound investments ensure they align with the country's political interest and overall economic development policy.
addition, CBRC and SASAC also play oversight role. Risk management guidelines issued the CBRC 2008 opened the door for Chinese banks provide loans for merger and acquisition purposes (previously forbidden under 1996 regulation.) They require "banks perform due diligence regarding compliance, operational, and commercial risks relating the parties and the transaction."12 There mention social and environmental risks.
Rather than seeking financing primarily through the capital markets, Chinese companies obtain 80-90% their funding from Chinese banks.13 part the Global strategy, China's state-owned policy banks, largely the Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) and the China Development Bank (CDB), were mobilized facilitate international capital flows and support mergers and acquisitions foreign companies. Although not the largest terms total assets and domestic investment, China Exim Bank and CDB play the leading role overseas investment. Other state-owned banks, such the export and credit insurance company (Sinosure), have also contributed lesser scale.
The Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) was formed 1994 along with two other "policy banks," the China Development Bank and the Agricultural Development Bank China, "as tools the government, allowing Beijing allocate preferential targeted finance through hybrid planning and market means."14 policy bank, China Exim finances and implements the government's trade and overseas investment policies.15 The Bank under the direct leadership the State Council.
China Exim has exhibited phenomenal growth over the past decade. has increased lending volumes 30% 40% year-on-year indicator the accelerating nature the "Go Global" strategy. China Exim far the largest export credit agency the world. approved over $70 billion new lending 2009, more than U.S. Exim, JBIC, and BNDES Exim combined.
Why China Going Global?
Increasing demand for energy and natural resources major driver behind China's foreign direct investments. 2010, China was the world's leading consumer several major commodities including copper, steel, coal, lead and iron ore. China's economy experiences unprecedented growth, there growing need for new markets, technology, and brands.
The bulk China's imports from Africa originate from relatively few countries. While investments are spread across African countries, over 70% Chinese OFDI stock 2008 was concentrated five resource rich countries: South Africa, Nigeria, Zambia, Sudan, and Algeria.
However, international comparison, China's investments Africa's natural resources match patterns. 50%-80% percent all FDI Africa goes natural resource exploitation.21 Despite the rapid scale-up Chinese investment Africa, most foreign direct investment (FDI) Africa originates from OECD countries 91.6% total inward FDI stock Africa 2008.22 Similarly, the bulk U.S. imports from Africa are sourced from relatively few resource rich countries; 77% total imports 2009 came from five countries: Nigeria (30.5%), Algeria (17.3%), Angola (15%,) South Africa (9.2%), and Congo-B. (4.9%).23 The top three African oil exporters Nigeria, Angola, Algeria for the U.S. and Angola, Sudan, Libya for China provided quarter each country's total imports (27.6% the U.S., 25.1% for China).
China has executed number deals Africa recent years, backed not just oil but also bauxite, chromium, iron ore, and even cocoa. these deals, China provides loans for infrastructure development, which are repaid delivery sales the borrowing country's natural resources. This structure used most commonly when country does not have the financial capacity guarantee and/or service loan commitment but has natural resource (such oil) offer repayment. This approach follows long history natural resource-based transactions and far from unique China.
BNDES originally Banco Nacional Desenvolvimento Economico Social (National Bank for Economic and Social Development) but rebranded "El banco desarrollo Brasil," ("The Brazilian Development Bank") wholly owned federal government company.26 the largest provider funding for capital investment Brazil. key source for long-term financing and subsidized interest rates, the bank normally responsible for almost 20% total credit granted Brazilian banks the private sector.
2009, BNDES estimates that provided nearly 40% financing for all investments Brazilian manufacturing and infrastructure.27 BNDES provides direct credit, fund distribution through financial intermediaries, and equity investment. also provides grants for social, cultural, and technological development. With total assets $285 billion (as September 2010), BNDES Brazil's fourth largest bank.
Not only did China displace the U.S. Brazil's biggest trade partner 2009, but was also Brazil's largest foreign investor, investing wide range areas, from iron ore mines vast tracts farmland and the electricity grid.
China and Brazil: Strategic Partnership?
China and Brazil seek similar relationships with Africa, sharing strong proclivity oil and mining activities. China's trade and investment relationship with Africa, however, far more diverse than Brazil's, encompassing energy, manufacturing, retail and agriculture addition oil and mining. This makes less likely that the two countries will compete over investment opportunities. key challenge ensure these trade flows result clear benefits the host government and local communities, and safeguard the environment.
With our record independent research, our experience convening wide range stakeholders, and our close partnerships with organizations China, Brazil, and several host countries Africa and Asia, WRI's International Financial Flows and the Environment (IFFE) initiative supports efforts both investors and host countries move towards environmentally and socially responsible development.
IFFE works help these countries apply the highest climate change, environmental and social standards their investment overseas, report publicly these standards, and respond the concerns NGOs and local communities.
For additional information WRI's Emerging Actors Development Finance work, contact WRI Senior Associate Xiaomei Tan xtan@wri.org.
Note: All currencies are quotes U.S. dollars unless otherwise stated.
This collection figures and charts based preliminary research conducted Bruce Jenkins, WRI consultant, and Xiaomei Tan, WRI Senior Associate. The scoping research concluded April 2011 and includes data from various sources that are updated frequently. The data circulated stimulate timely discussion and critical feedback and influence ongoing debate emerging issues. WRI has not verified the data, and figures and charts are meant used for illustrative purposes. WRI will continue update the data our research moves forward.

The landscape development finance changing rapidly. Traditionally, international financial flows moved from developed countries developing countries. the last decade, however, major emerging economies such China and Brazil have fueled growing trend South-South development flows increasingly channeling their overseas investments other developing countries.
China and Brazil are surfacing major international investors through nationally owned financial institutions such the Export-Import Bank China, the China Development Bank and the Brazilian Development Bank (BNDES). These "emerging actors" are financing major initiatives acquire natural resources, open markets, and forge strategic political ties. They are increasingly financing large-scale, high impact projects beyond their borders such hydropower plants and gas pipelines which may pose new challenges for environmental and social sustainability.
This preliminary research focuses Chinese and Brazilian overseas investments and begins look the growth drivers and geographic trends those investments.
Expanding South-South trade and investment provides welcome and needed sources capital for countries Africa, Asia, and Latin America. the same time, these financial flows coupled with the emergence powerful financial actors from China, India, Brazil, and other economies may pose new challenges for environmental and social sustainability.
Trends indicate that developing economies will "account for 57% world GDP [Gross Domestic Product] 2030."3 Despite sharp differences among members, the G-20 supplanting the G-8 the primary vehicle for global economic policy coordination.4 Large emerging market economies are defining their own approaches development cooperation, governance issues, and environmental and social sustainability outside many existing normative frameworks.
2001, China's tenth Five-Year Plan (2001-2005) formalized the directive for Chinese companies "Go Global," strategy gain access needed resources, stimulate the export goods, and grow China's multinational businesses and brands. Beijing has provided diplomatic support, favorable tax exemptions, insurance, and, critically, access low-cost finance.
Chinese authorities have simplified regulations facilitate investment abroad. Three governmental bodies MOFCOM, SAFE, and the NDRC have primary but not sole oversight China's overseas investment (separate from foreign assistance) regime. MOFCOM responsible for developing regulations for outbound investment and for coordinating activities with commercial counselors posted Chinese embassies. SAFE issued new regulations 2009 that reduced qualification requirements for offshore foreign currency lending and expanded the sources funds for lending (including access government foreign exchange reserves).11 The NDRC reviews large outbound investments ensure they align with the country's political interest and overall economic development policy.
addition, CBRC and SASAC also play oversight role. Risk management guidelines issued the CBRC 2008 opened the door for Chinese banks provide loans for merger and acquisition purposes (previously forbidden under 1996 regulation.) They require "banks perform due diligence regarding compliance, operational, and commercial risks relating the parties and the transaction."12 There mention social and environmental risks.
Rather than seeking financing primarily through the capital markets, Chinese companies obtain 80-90% their funding from Chinese banks.13 part the Global strategy, China's state-owned policy banks, largely the Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) and the China Development Bank (CDB), were mobilized facilitate international capital flows and support mergers and acquisitions foreign companies. Although not the largest terms total assets and domestic investment, China Exim Bank and CDB play the leading role overseas investment. Other state-owned banks, such the export and credit insurance company (Sinosure), have also contributed lesser scale.
The Export-Import Bank China (China Exim) was formed 1994 along with two other "policy banks," the China Development Bank and the Agricultural Development Bank China, "as tools the government, allowing Beijing allocate preferential targeted finance through hybrid planning and market means."14 policy bank, China Exim finances and implements the government's trade and overseas investment policies.15 The Bank under the direct leadership the State Council.
China Exim has exhibited phenomenal growth over the past decade. has increased lending volumes 30% 40% year-on-year indicator the accelerating nature the "Go Global" strategy. China Exim far the largest export credit agency the world. approved over $70 billion new lending 2009, more than U.S. Exim, JBIC, and BNDES Exim combined.

original text:
part its region-wide initiative support States with the safe and secure management conventional ammunition, the United Nations Regional Centre for Peace and Disarmament Asia and the Pacific organized the Southeast Asia Regional Outreach Seminar Trade and Trafficking Illicit Conventional Ammunition, Bangkok, Thailand, from 20-22 March 2019.
response the challenges posed unsecured poorly managed national ammunition stockpiles, the Seminar fostered regional dialogue between Southeast Asian States matters conventional ammunition, and provided forum share practices and engage with experts how address these challenges. Participants examined current regional trends illicit trafficking ammunition, well effective practices and further steps address related risks and challenges regional security. The Seminar also strengthened regional cooperation and coordination between national law-enforcement agencies and regional entities dealing with such matters.
The presentations and discussions incorporated range perspectives, and tools like the International Ammunition Technical Guidelines and the SaferGuard platform, and examined the implications diversion and illicit trafficking safety and security, well possible joint responses and synergies between global frameworks and instruments.
The Seminar further allocated whole day support the work upcoming United Nations group governmental experts problems arising from the accumulation conventional ammunition surplus, convened 2020 accordance with General Assembly resolution 72/55.
The Seminar contribution the implementation the Secretary General's Disarmament Agenda, "Securing Our Common Future," specifically realizing Action securing excessive and poorly maintained stockpiles. The Seminar also promoted the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, particularly Goal peace, justice and strong institutions.
The Seminar was made possible with financial support from the Government Germany.
For further information, please contact Yuriy Kryvonos, Director the United Nations Regional Centre for Peace and Disarmament Asia and the Pacific, via e-mail info@unrcpd.org.
